summary.overall_summary: This option creates a smooth, full-arc Japan-to-Tokyo experience by anchoring your trip with a nonstop outbound flight into an overnight hotel, then layering sightseeing with meal breaks that keep transfers manageable. The days are arranged so each morning starts with a garden or landmark, lunch transitions you into the next cluster, and each evening pairing either closes into the hotel stay (arrival) or ends the sightseeing block (departure day).

day_summaries.Day 1: Outbound flight, then dinner at RollSloth🦥, then overnight at OHANA TOKYO HOUSE. Keeping the sequence flight → dinner → hotel works as a single continuous arrival rhythm, with dinner positioned to cleanly close into the overnight anchor.
day_summaries.Day 2: Meiji Jingu → MO-MO-PARADISE Shibuya Koendori → Zojo-ji Temple → Narisawa. Lunch at MO-MO-PARADISE Shibuya Koendori bridges the morning and afternoon sightseeing blocks without a major detour, and the order leaves Narisawa as a dinner closer after Zojo-ji Temple.
day_summaries.Day 3: Kiyosumi Gardens → Gyukatsu Ichinisan → Edo-Tokyo Museum → Tokyo Tower → T’s Tantan. The plan uses Gyukatsu Ichinisan as the lunch bridge after Kiyosumi Gardens, then keeps the Edo-Tokyo Museum and Tokyo Tower pair on the same day to maintain a continuous afternoon run even with a longer transfer, finishing with T’s Tantan as the dinner close.
day_summaries.Day 4: Koishikawa Korakuen → CENSU TOKYO → Shibuya Sky → Grilled Wagyu Beef. Lunch at CENSU TOKYO sits between the morning and afternoon blocks to avoid a major detour, and Grilled Wagyu Beef is placed after Shibuya Sky to end the day’s sightseeing run with enough breathing room before the next anchor.
day_summaries.Day 5: Checkout from OHANA TOKYO HOUSE → Rikugien Gardens → the return nonstop flight. Starting with checkout anchors the departure day, Rikugien Gardens follows as a morning activity after the overnight stay, and the nonstop return flight closes the itinerary as the final anchor with enough breathing room before departure.
